161143.name: Dru
161143.notes_on_life_history: Failed breeding attempt 2019 N'land - successful 2020 N'land, failed predation 2021 N'land.
161143.notes_on_loss: Killed on nest defending 4 chicks c25d.o. 12/06/22, transmitted mortality fix 13/06/22. At least 3 of 4 chicks fledged successfully. Final transmission location withheld; sensitive nest site.

162148.name: Sorrel
162148.notes_on_life_history: Bred successfully 2019-2020 Cairngorms, failed Cairngorms 2021 - post failure flew north to Papa Westray (behaviour associated with predation event at nest) then south to Cumbria, returned north to Caithness then south again to Cumbria in June. Failed breeding in 2022 and 2023, bred successfully 2024 and 2025.

162150a.name: Fortune

183701.notes_on_life_history: Bird wintered in France, MFU soon after return to UK

183703.name: R1-F1-19
183703.notes_on_loss: Possible tag failure: In 2021 and 2022 an unidentifiable female fitted with a Lotek transmitter was recorded breeding in Northumberland and Bowland respectively.

183704.name: R1-M2-19
183704.notes_on_life_history: Bird observed with bent aerial on tag after tagging on 25/07, revisit on 30/07, recaptured and fitted with new tag

201117.name: Asta
201117.notes_on_loss: Recovered - persecution. Tag recovered attached to remains of carrion crow, in days before recovery the bird had an established home range in the N. Pennines around Gilmonby Moor

201118.location_raw: LKF site confidential near goshawk nest
201118.name: N/A
201118.notes_on_loss: Thought to be natural death caused by Goshawk predation.Transmitter and ringed leg recovered under Goshawk nest in July 2021 in proximity of last known fix. No transmissions from tag for >12 months due to it being either embedded in nest and/or under canopy. Goshawk predation was suspected when the tag stopped transmitting in 2020. Final transmission location withheld; sensitive Goshawk nest site.

201119.name: Solo
201119.notes_on_loss: Final transmission location withheld; sensitive nest site.

201121.name: Free
201121.notes_on_life_history: Colin's son - non-breeder in 2021, frequented Yorkshire Dales and Cumbria in summer 2021 and into spring in 2022.
201121.notes_on_loss: Recovered - persecution. Bird found decapitated and with ringed leg removed.

201122.name: Susie
201122.notes_on_life_history: 2021 - Bred Bowland, failed breeding in 2022 in YDNP due to human persecution, chicks trampled in nest. 2023 - bred successfully fledged 5
201122.notes_on_loss: Confirmed dead, had been victim of shooting. When found dead remains would not allow determination of whether the bird died as a result of it being shot. Bird died between 25/11/2023 and 12/02/2024.

201123.name: Ned
201123.notes_on_loss: First listed as MFU in December 2020, intermittent transmissions through to July 21, restarted in Feb 22 - bird dead in-situ.

201125.name: Ingerid
201125.notes_on_loss: First listed as MFU in November 2020, intermittent transmissions through to Sep 21 - bird dead in-situ.

201126.location_raw: Site confidential near goshawk and peregrine nest site
201126.name: N/A
201126.notes_on_loss: Remains of bird and tag recovered near nest site post fledging - most likely avian predation. Final transmission location withheld; sensitive Goshawk and Peregrine nest site.

201126a.name: Lagertha
201126a.notes_on_loss: Last fix is plotted over built up area as it is of low accuracy, bird recorded roosting at nearby estuary

203003.name: R2-F1-20
203003.notes_on_life_history: 2021 - Failed breeding attempt, likely predation Cumbria

203004.name: R1-M2-20
203004.notes_on_life_history: Frank's son - 2021 and 2022 - bred successfully, Co Durham
203004.notes_on_loss: Tag stopped transmitting 12 June 2023 while attemting to breed with 2 females

203005.name: R2-F3-20
203005.notes_on_life_history: 2021 and 2022 - Bred successfully with sibling 55146 Co Durham. Bred with untagged male 2023, 2024 and 2025 (Scottish Borders)

213843.name: Pete
213843.notes_on_life_history: 2023 - Home range established in SW Scotland after return from wintering in France. Low vole counts in SW Scotland offered a possible reason why Pete and other tagged birds in the area never bred. Wintered in same range and roost sites in France as in previous years. Transmission stopped in May 2025, ground search unsuccessful ongoing.

213845.location_raw: LKF site confidential near goshawk nest
213845.name: Lydia
213845.notes_on_loss: Final transmission location withheld; sensitive Goshawk nest site.

213846.name: Amelia
213846.notes_on_loss: Possible tag failure: Female with satellite transmitter identified several kms away from Amelia's tags last known fix in Bowland, bred successfully with no associated data.

213847.name: Percy
213847.notes_on_loss: Likely natural death. Recovered by RSPB Scotland 22/04/22

213849.name: Val
213849.notes_on_loss: Likely natural death. Recovered - 23/05/22 - Likely predation and secondary scavenged, tag restarted transmitting a mortality signal in spring 2022 enabling recovery using scanning equipment

213851.name: Rodney
213851.notes_on_life_history: Wintered in same range in France as previous years

213920.location_raw: Site confidential hen harrier roost site
213920.name: R2-M2-21
213920.notes_on_loss: Natural death. Final transmission location withheld; sensitive roost site. Last transmission 27/10/21 recovered 28/10/21

232636.name: R1-F4-22
232636.notes_on_loss: Retransmission following bright weather in April 2025. Cause of death inconclusive.

232638.location_raw: Site confidential hen harrier breeding site
232638.name: R1-F2-22
232638.notes_on_loss: Presumed tag failure. Bird breeding and photographed into mid May at breeding site with damaged aerial, aerial almost detached from transmitter hanging at side of bird, transmitter and harness in place. Bird alive after last transmission. Final transmission location withheld; sensitive nest site.

232639.name: R1-F3-22
232639.notes_on_loss: Previously listed as transmitting May 2023 in due to intermittent transmissions.

232640.location_raw: Site confidential hen harrier breeding site
232640.name: R1-F1-22

234074.name: Bernie
234074.notes_on_life_history: Bred successfully 2023 (7 eggs, fledged 6), 2024 and 2025 N'land, tag is not-recharging in low light conditions.

234075.location_raw: Site confidential near goshawk nest site
234075.name: Jenny
234075.notes_on_loss: Final transmission location withheld; sensitive Goshawk nest site.

234076.location_raw: Site confidential hen harrier breeding site
234076.name: Reuben
234076.notes_on_loss: Failed to thrive, did not leave natal area, tag transmitted one weak fix Oct 22. Final transmission location withheld; sensitive nest site.

234077.name: Craig
234077.notes_on_life_history: Attempted to breed near natal site in Peak District - failed reasons unknown

234079.name: Penelope
234079.notes_on_life_history: *Tag data transmissions intermittent from fledging, possibly related to preened feather obscuring solar panel. 2023 Tag data delivery improved with mainly Doppler transmissions, bred successfully fledging 6 young from 7 eggs. 2024 tag issue continues, a bird matching Penelope's description photographed near 2023 breeding site with no accompanying tracking data. The last satellite data transmitted in March 2024, but tagged bird thought to be Penelope was seen alive in May 2024.

240294.name: Gill
240294.notes_on_life_history: Gill was listed as MFU 27/11/23. Further investigations proved Gill was alive at the site of her LKF and transmissions since have shown this was an issue with the tag not recharging sufficiently through the particulalrly wet and dark winter months. Bred successfully 2024 Lanarkshire. Seen regularly throughout December 2024.

254837.name: Dina
254837.notes_on_loss: The remains consisted of dry feathers and bones only. The phalangeal bones were noted to be intact and associated with primary feathers, the carpometacarpal bone from both limbs were fractured; all other bones were fragments only. It is possible that the carcass had been extensively scavenged after death – the ends of the bone fragments identified were rough and uneven - and it was impossible to rule out other traumatic aetiologies, nor is it possible to establish whether the fractures happened before or after death.

254838.name: Mary
254838.notes_on_life_history: Mary 254838 fledged from a nest near Otterburn Northumberland in 2025 then moved to an area in the Scottish Borders.
254838.notes_on_loss:
Recovered - unknown.

Postmortem report summary:
This juvenile hen harrier is likely to have died acutely given the recent ingestion of two meals. The main pathological findings were haemorrhage in both the skull and sternum, the cause of which cannot be determined given the level of autolysis and decomposition of the carcass. Ventriculus perforation and/or rodenticide ingestion remain possibilities, but the state of carcass decomposition precludes diagnosis.

Further toxicological investigation therefore followed the postmortem. The analytical investigation failed to provide any evidence to implicate pesticides poisoning with the death of this hen harrier. No residues of contaminants throught most likely to be associated with this incident were detected.

No cause of death can be identified. Classed as unknown and case closed for further investigations at this time.

254843.notes_on_loss:
Recovered - unknown.

The recovered carcase was not suitable for post-mortem owing to level of decomposition. Whilst ballistic analysis showed the tag had been struck by a lead projectile, no projectiles were detected in the carcase and the cause of death could not be determined

254844.name: Margaret
254844.notes_on_loss: Suspected illegally killed. Tag found removed. Carcase not found.

263531.name: Murphy
263531.notes_on_loss:
Recovered - natural.

Postmortem report summary:
The most significant finding was the absence of subcutaneous, visceral and pericardial fat, and moderate muscle atrophy. Together, these indicate emaciation, suggesting chronic disease or starvation as the most likely causes of death. There was no evidence of trauma.

263532.name: Patricia
263532.notes_on_loss:
Recovered - unknown

The recovered carcase was not suitable for post-mortem owing to level of decomposition. However, predation or failure to thrive appear likely (predator sign evident, tag undamaged).

34345.name: Frank
34345.notes_on_life_history: Bred Successfully 2019 -2020 (Polygynous) and 2021 (Polygynous) Yorkshire Dales. Bred successfully 2022 (live nestcam) 2023 (polygynous), 2024 and 2025. Tag stopped transmitting suddenly in May 2025, bird OK provisioning young into August, image taken by NE field staff shows aerial missing. Recovered January 2026.
34345.notes_on_loss:
Recovered - HPAI

Tag stopped transmitting due to aerial missing May 2025.

Frank's body recovered incidentally January 2026 -- initial X ray returned no obvious foreign objects within body; tested positive for HPAI so full postmortem not proceedable per Health & Safety Executive (HSE) rules to protect human health.

34346.name: Sofia
34346.notes_on_life_history: Tag intermittent in poor light, transmitting approx every 22 days in winter months, bird known to roost in trees, Bred successfully 2019-2020 and failed due to predation 2021. Failed breeding 2022, 2023 and successful 2024

55144.name: R2-F2-20
55144.notes_on_life_history: 2021 and 2022 - Bred successfully Bowland with RSPB tagged male "Heath"

55146a.name: R2-M1-20
55146a.notes_on_life_history: 2021 and 2022 - Bred successfully with sibling 203005 Co Durham

55153.name: R1-M3-20
55153.notes_on_life_history: Frank's son - 2021 - no breeding, 2022 - bred successfully.

57255.name: Kelly
57255.notes_on_loss: Possible tag failure: transmissions intermittent up until last known fix, large error radius. In 2021 and 2022 an unidentifiable female fitted with a Lotek transmitter was recorded breeding in Northumberland and Bowland respectively. In August 2023, a bird was photographed in County Durham resembling this bird. It was possible to identify a BTO ring on left leg and a second generation Lotek transmitter meaning this can only be one of two birds (Harriet or Kelly).

57265.name: Colin
57265.notes_on_life_history: Bred successfully in 2021 and 2020 in Northumberland and Cumbria respectively. Last known transmission at site where he eventually bred in 2021, seen frequently and photographed around nest. Colin's tag known to transmit intermittently. Survived predation event as a point of fledge nestling that killed 3 of his siblings. Wintered in W.Pennies as yearling and was recorded there witout associated ata. Bred 2022 identified by GPS tag with one aerial snapped.
57265.notes_on_loss: Likely tag failure: subsequently identified from photographs. Final transmission location withheld; sensitive nest site.

57266.name: Alex
57266.notes_on_loss: Natural death. Remains recovered 15/08/19, most likely predation

57266#row24.name: Harriet
57266#row24.notes_on_loss: Possible tag failure: In 2021 and 2022 an unidentifiable female fitted with a Lotek transmitter was recorded breeding in Northumberland and Bowland respectively. In August 2023 a bird was photographed in County Durham resembling this bird. It was possible to identify a BTO ring on left leg and a second generation Lotek transmitter meaning this can only be one of two birds (Harriet or Kelly).

57273.name: Teddy
57273.notes_on_life_history: Nest located post fledging, bird captured and fitted with transmitter in nest vicinity.
57273.notes_on_loss: Possible tag failure: In 2021 and 2022 an unidentifiable female fitted with a Lotek transmitter was recorded breeding in Northumberland and Bowland respectively.

57278.name: Rosie
57278.notes_on_loss: Possible tag failure: In 2021 and 2022 an unidentifiable female fitted with a Lotek transmitter was recorded breeding in Northumberland and Bowland respectively. Final transmission location withheld; sensitive roost site

57280.name: N/A
57280.notes_on_loss: Bird fledged then returned to the nest and was predated along with 2 of its siblings. The fourth chick (Colin above) survived. Final transmission location withheld; sensitive nest site.
